title: Indefinite hang on macOS ARM when running podman run --platform linux/amd64 -it fedora sh
body:
### Issue Description

Hang with podman 5.7.1 on Apple ARM/M2 Pro running macOS Tahoe 26.1 when running the AMD64 Fedora image and trying to start a shell:

```
$ podman run --rm --platform linux/amd64 -it fedora sh
```

Ctrl^C doesn't work and I have to kill it:

```
$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                     COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS         PORTS       NAMES
ce488635826c  registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest  sh          53 seconds ago  Up 54 seconds              objective_black
$ podman kill ce488635826c
ce488635826c
```

However, running the `uname` command works fine:

```
$ podman run --rm --platform linux/amd64 -it fedora uname -m
x86_64
```

On the native ARM platform things work fine:

```
$ podman run --rm --platform linux/arm64 -it fedora sh
sh-5.3# 
```

I recreated the `podman machine` without luck and also tried `export CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER=applehv` (recreated the machine), neither of which helped.

Other AMD64 images work fine and I can start a shell, so it seems to be something about the Fedora image:

```
$ podman run --rm --platform linux/amd64 -it icr.io/appcafe/ibm-semeru-runtimes:certified-21-jdk-ubi sh
sh-4.4$ 
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman run --rm --platform linux/amd64 -it fedora sh`


### Describe the results you received

Hang and shell doesn't start

### Describe the results you expected

Start Fedora image with a shell

### Additional information

comments.body: Please check [issue #27799](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27799) and read [this blog post](https://blog.podman.io/2025/08/podman-5-6-released-rosetta-status-update/). It seems like Rosetta is not activated in the VM.

@Honny1 Thanks, that was it! Adding `rosetta=true` to `~/.config/containers/containers.conf` and re-creating the `podman machine` didn't help, but following the steps in [the blog post](https://blog.podman.io/2025/08/podman-5-6-released-rosetta-status-update/) fixed it.
